summary: The A45 AMG's M133 engine produces incredible power for a 2.0L four-cylinder, but this level of performance from a small displacement engine comes with higher stress and maintenance demands.

issues.name: Turbo Failure
issues.description: The M133's turbocharger operates under extreme conditions to produce its power output. Turbo bearing failure can occur, especially on cars driven hard without proper warm-up/cool-down.

issues.name: Injector Failure
issues.description: The direct fuel injectors can develop leaks or become clogged, causing rough running, misfires, and potential fuel smell in the cabin.

issues.name: DCT Clutch Wear
issues.description: The 7-speed dual-clutch transmission's clutch packs can wear prematurely under aggressive driving. Shuddering at low speeds is the early warning sign.

issues.name: Carbon Buildup
issues.description: Direct injection causes carbon deposits on intake valves. The M133's high boost levels can accelerate carbon accumulation.

issues.name: Interior Trim Rattles
issues.description: The W176 A-Class platform was not originally designed for AMG-level performance. Interior trim pieces can develop rattles from the engine vibration and stiff suspension.

buyingTips: Allow the turbo to warm up before hard driving and cool down before shutting off — turbo longevity depends on it
The M133 engine needs quality synthetic oil changed every 10,000 miles — do not extend intervals
DCT fluid change every 40,000 miles is essential — Mercedes does not include this in their standard service schedule
Carbon cleaning at 40,000 miles is recommended — the M133 builds carbon faster than less stressed engines
The A45 was not officially sold in the US — most examples are imports. Verify import documentation and compliance
